sp13-stage-2-5-import-plan.preamble: Before you review the generated import plan in detail at T-7, ratify the rationale behind the proposed taxonomy. The plan you are about to see is the OUTPUT of density-based clustering (HDBSCAN) followed by LLM-assisted iterative refinement (TnT-LLM pattern: Pass 1 propose, Pass 2 outlier merge/split). This consultation gate surfaces WHY the candidates are grouped and named the way they are, and the tradeoffs the system accepted on your behalf — so you can question the underlying logic before rubber-stamping the plan.
sp13-stage-2-5-import-plan.tradeoffs: Discoverability vs flexibility. Comprehensive coverage vs cognitive cap on top-level structures. Late retrofit of structural decisions (tag prefixes, folder shape, frontmatter contracts) consumes 50-70% of migration project budgets per industry data — favouring a smaller, MECE taxonomy at the cost of some coverage. Status-quo bias avoidance: reviewing the rendered plan WITHOUT ratifying its underlying rationale risks accepting cluster boundaries that don't actually match your work patterns (CHI 2024 PMC review).

citations.author: Cowan, N.
citations.title: The magical number 4 in short-term memory: A reconsideration of mental storage capacity
citations.claim: Working-memory cap of 4 ± 1 — modern replacement for Miller 7±2; supports keeping top-level taxonomy at single digits.

citations.author: Forte, T.
citations.title: PARA: A Universal System for Digital Information Management
citations.claim: PARA's 4 categories (Projects/Areas/Resources/Archives) anchored on Cowan-4 + Hick's Law decision-time scaling — convergent industry evidence for low-cardinality top-level taxonomies.

citations.author: Schmidt, J. (Zettelkasten.de on Luhmann)
citations.title: Luhmann's mature Zettelkasten and the 100-to-11 collapse
citations.claim: Luhmann's mature slip-box collapsed top-level categories from 100+ to 11 over decades — convergent empirical evidence for the Cowan-4 cap as a ceiling, not a floor.

citations.author: Hick, W. E. & Hyman, R.
citations.title: On the rate of gain of information (Hick's Law)
citations.claim: Decision time scales logarithmically with the number of choices: T = b·log2(n+1). Every additional top-level category multiplies the user-decision-time on every routing decision.

citations.author: Wan, M. et al. (Microsoft Research)
citations.title: TnT-LLM: Text Mining at Scale with LLMs
citations.claim: Iterative LLM-driven taxonomy refinement (Pass 1 propose, Pass 2 outlier merge/split) — the canonical pattern SP13 T-5 propose-taxonomy implements.

citations.author: Campello, R. J. G. B. et al.
citations.title: Density-Based Clustering Based on Hierarchical Density Estimates (HDBSCAN)
citations.claim: HDBSCAN's noise/outlier semantics — items in low-density regions are explicitly classified as 'unclassified' rather than force-assigned. Source of the unclassified-pile call-out in the import plan.
